revision.comment: [[WP:AES|←]]新しいページ: ''''太祝詞神社'''（ふとのりとじんじゃ）は、[[長崎県]][[対馬市]][[美津島町]]加志に鎮座する[[神社]]である。旧県社。 か...'
revision.text:
'''太祝詞神社'''（ふとのりとじんじゃ）は、[[長崎県]][[対馬市]][[美津島町]]加志に鎮座する[[神社]]である。旧県社。
かつては加志大明神・賀志宮とも称した。
==祭神== *太詔戸神（太祝詞神） *雷大臣命 - 津島直の祖神とされる。
==歴史== [[806年]]（大同元年）の「新抄格勅符抄」に「太祝詞神 二戸対馬島」とあり、封戸二戸が与えられたとある。
[[延喜式神名帳]]には「太祝詞（ふとのりとの）神社」とあり、名神大社とされている。
==境内== *雷大臣命の墓と伝わる壇がある。
==参考文献== *『長崎県の地名』（郷土歴史大事典 日本歴史地名大系43）、平凡社、2001年 ISBN 4-582-49043-3
==関連項目== *[[森神社 (天理市)]] - かつて「太祝詞神社」と称した。 *[[祝詞神社]] - 長崎県佐世保市に鎮座する神社。
==外部リンク== *[http://www.genbu.net/data/tusima/futonorito_title.htm 太祝詞神社（玄松子）]
{{神道 横}} {{DEFAULTSORT:ふとのりとしんしや}} [[Category:長崎県の神社]] [[Category:名神大社]] [[Category:府県社]] [[Category:対馬市]] {{shinto-stub}}

'''太祝詞神社'''（ふとのりとじんじゃ）は、[[長崎県]][[対馬市]][[美津島町]]加志に鎮座する[[神社]]である。旧県社。
かつては加志大明神・賀志宮とも称した。
==祭神== *太詔戸神（太祝詞神） *雷大臣命 - 津島直の祖神とされる。
==歴史== [[806年]]（大同元年）の「新抄格勅符抄」に「太祝詞神 二戸対馬島」とあり、封戸二戸が与えられたとある。
[[延喜式神名帳]]には「太祝詞（ふとのりとの）神社」とあり、[[名神大社]]とされている。
==境内== *雷大臣命の墓と伝わる壇がある。
==参考文献== *『長崎県の地名』（郷土歴史大事典 日本歴史地名大系43）、平凡社、2001年 ISBN 4-582-49043-3
==関連項目== *[[森神社 (天理市)]] - かつて「太祝詞神社」と称した。 *[[祝詞神社]] - 長崎県[[佐世保市]]に鎮座する神社。
==外部リンク== *[http://www.genbu.net/data/tusima/futonorito_title.htm 太祝詞神社（玄松子）]
{{神道 横}} {{DEFAULTSORT:ふとのりとしんしや}} [[Category:長崎県の神社]] [[Category:名神大社]] [[Category:府県社]] [[Category:対馬市]] {{shinto-stub}}

{{神社 |名称 = 太祝詞神社 |画像 = [[画像:Gazoubosyu.jpg]] |所在地 = 長崎県対馬市美津島町加志512 |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|16|44.1|N|129|14|23.8|E|scale:25000}} |祭神 = 太詔戸神・雷大臣命 |社格 = 式内大社（名神大）・旧県社 |創建 = |本殿 = [[神明造]] |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = 陰暦9月9日 |神事 = }}
'''太祝詞神社'''（ふとのりとじんじゃ）は、[[長崎県]][[対馬市]][[美津島町]]加志に鎮座する[[神社]]である。[[旧県社]]。
かつては加志大明神・賀志宮とも称した。
== 祭神 == 太詔戸神（太祝詞神）と[[雷大臣命]]を祀る。
太詔戸神は[[天児屋命]]の事とされ、雷大臣命は[[津島氏 (神別)|津島直]]の祖神とされる。
== 歴史 == [[大同 (日本)|大同]]元年（[[806年]]）の『[[新抄格勅符抄]]』に「太祝詞神 二戸対馬島」とあり、[[対馬島]]で[[神封戸]]2戸が与えられたとある。
『[[延喜式神名帳]]』には「太祝詞神社」とあり、[[名神大社]]とされている。
== 境内 == 雷大臣命の墓と伝わる壇がある。
== 参考文献 == *『長崎県の地名』（日本歴史地名大系43）、平凡社、2001年ISBN 4-582-49043-3
== 関連項目 == *[[森神社 (天理市)]] - かつて「太祝詞神社」と称した。 *[[祝詞神社]] - 長崎県[[佐世保市]]に鎮座する神社。
== 外部リンク == *[http://www.genbu.net/data/tusima/futonorito_title.htm 太祝詞神社]（玄松子「玄松子の記憶」（個人サイト））
{{DEFAULTSORT:ふとのりとしんしや}} [[Category:対馬市の神社]] [[Category:名神大社]] [[Category:長崎県の旧県社]] {{shinto-stub}}

{{神社 |名称 = 太祝詞神社 |画像 = [[画像:Gazoubosyu.jpg]] |所在地 = 長崎県対馬市美津島町加志512 |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|16|44.1|N|129|14|23.8|E|scale:25000}} |祭神 = 太詔戸神・雷大臣命 |社格 = 式内大社（名神大）・旧県社 |創建 = |本殿 = [[神明造]] |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = 陰暦9月9日 |神事 = }}
'''太祝詞神社'''（ふとのりとじんじゃ）は、[[長崎県]][[対馬市]][[美津島町]]加志に鎮座する[[神社]]である。[[旧県社]]。
かつては加志大明神・賀志宮とも称した。
== 祭神 == 太詔戸神（太祝詞神）と[[雷大臣命]]を祀る。
太詔戸神は[[天児屋命]]の事とされ、雷大臣命は[[津島氏 (神別)|津島直]]の祖神とされる。
== 歴史 == [[大同 (日本)|大同]]元年（[[806年]]）の『[[新抄格勅符抄]]』に「太祝詞神 二戸対馬島」とあり、[[対馬島]]で[[神封戸]]2戸が与えられたとある。
『[[延喜式神名帳]]』には「太祝詞神社」とあり、[[名神大社]]とされている。
== 境内 == 雷大臣命の墓と伝わる壇がある。
== 参考文献 == *『長崎県の地名』（日本歴史地名大系43）、平凡社、2001年ISBN 4-582-49043-3
== 関連項目 == *[[森神社 (天理市)]] - かつて「太祝詞神社」と称した。 *[[祝詞神社]] - 長崎県[[佐世保市]]に鎮座する神社。
== 外部リンク == *[http://www.genbu.net/data/tusima/futonorito_title.htm 太祝詞神社]（玄松子「玄松子の記憶」（個人サイト））

{{神社 |名称 = 太祝詞神社 |画像 = [[ファイル:Hutonorito jinja Haiden.JPG|250px|拝殿]]
拝殿 |所在地 = 長崎県対馬市美津島町加志512 |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|16|44.1|N|129|14|23.8|E|scale:25000}} |祭神 = 太詔戸神・雷大臣命 |社格 = 式内社（名神大）・旧県社 |創建 = |本殿 = [[流造]] |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = 陰暦9月9日 |神事 = }} [[ファイル:Futonorito jinja Gate.JPG|thumb|right|250px|鳥居]] [[ファイル:Futonorito jinja Honden.JPG|thumb|right|250px|本殿]] '''太祝詞神社'''（ふとのりとじんじゃ）は、[[長崎県]][[対馬市]][[美津島町]]加志に鎮座する[[神社]]である。[[旧県社]]で、かつては加志大明神・賀志宮とも称した。 == 祭神 == 太詔戸神（太祝詞神）と[[雷大臣命]]を祀る。 太詔戸神は[[天児屋命]]の事とされ、雷大臣命は[[津島氏 (神別)|津島直]]の祖神とされる。 == 歴史 == [[大同 (日本)|大同]]元年（[[806年]]）の『[[新抄格勅符抄]]』に「太祝詞神　二戸対馬島」とあり、[[対馬島]]で[[神封戸]]2戸が与えられたとある。 [[延長 (元号)|延長]]5年（[[927年]]）の『[[延喜式神名帳]]』には「貞タ太祝詞神社　名神　大」とあり、[[名神大社]]に列せられている。 == 境内 == 雷大臣命の墓と伝わる壇がある。 == 参考文献 == *『長崎県の地名』（日本歴史地名大系43）、平凡社、2001年ISBN 4-582-49043-3 == 関連項目 == *[[森神社 (天理市)]] - かつて「太祝詞神社」と称した。 *[[祝詞神社]] - 長崎県[[佐世保市]]に鎮座する神社。 == 外部リンク == *[http://www.genbu.net/data/tusima/futonorito_title.htm 太祝詞神社]（玄松子「玄松子の記憶」（個人サイト）） {{commonscat|Futonorito-jinja}} {{DEFAULTSORT:ふとのりとしんしや}} [[Category:対馬市の神社]] [[Category:名神大社]] [[Category:長崎県の旧県社]] {{shinto-stub}}

revision.comment: /* 関連項目 */ 式内社のカテゴリ追加。
revision.text:
{{神社 |名称 = 太祝詞神社 |画像 = [[ファイル:Hutonorito jinja Haiden.JPG|250px|拝殿]]
拝殿 |所在地 = 長崎県対馬市美津島町加志512 |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|16|44.1|N|129|14|23.8|E|scale:25000}} |祭神 = 太詔戸神・雷大臣命 |社格 = 式内社（名神大）・旧県社 |創建 = |本殿 = [[流造]] |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = 陰暦9月9日 |神事 = }} [[ファイル:Futonorito jinja Gate.JPG|thumb|right|250px|鳥居]] [[ファイル:Futonorito jinja Honden.JPG|thumb|right|250px|本殿]] '''太祝詞神社'''（ふとのりとじんじゃ）は、[[長崎県]][[対馬市]]美津島町加志に鎮座する[[神社]]である。[[旧県社]]で、かつては加志大明神・賀志宮とも称した。 == 祭神 == 太詔戸神（太祝詞神）と[[雷大臣命]]を祀る。 太詔戸神は[[天児屋命]]の事とされ、雷大臣命は[[津島氏 (神別)|津島直]]の祖神とされる。 == 歴史 == [[806年]]（[[大同 (日本)|大同]]元年）の『[[新抄格勅符抄]]』に「太祝詞神 二戸対馬島」とあり、[[対馬島]]で[[神封戸]]2戸が与えられたとある。 [[927年]]（[[延長 (元号)|延長]]5年）の『[[延喜式神名帳]]』には「貞タ太祝詞神社 名神 大」とあり、[[名神大社]]に列せられている。 == 境内 == 雷大臣命の墓と伝わる壇がある。 == 参考文献 == *『長崎県の地名』（日本歴史地名大系43）、平凡社、2001年ISBN 4-582-49043-3 == 関連項目 == *[[森神社 (天理市)]] - かつて「太祝詞神社」と称した。 *[[祝詞神社]] - 長崎県[[佐世保市]]に鎮座する神社。 {{commonscat|Futonorito-jinja}} {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:ふとのりとしんしや}} [[Category:対馬市の神社]] [[Category:名神大社]] [[Category:対馬国の式内社|名ふとのりとしんしや]] [[Category:長崎県の旧県社]]

{{神社 |名称 = 太祝詞神社 |画像 = [[ファイル:Hutonorito jinja Haiden.JPG|250px|拝殿]]
拝殿 |所在地 = 長崎県対馬市美津島町加志512 |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|16|44.1|N|129|14|23.8|E|scale:25000}} |祭神 = 太詔戸神・雷大臣命 |社格 = 式内社（名神大）・旧県社 |創建 = |本殿 = [[流造]] |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = 陰暦9月9日 |神事 = }} [[ファイル:Futonorito jinja Gate.JPG|thumb|right|250px|鳥居]] [[ファイル:Futonorito jinja Honden.JPG|thumb|right|250px|本殿]] '''太祝詞神社'''（ふとのりとじんじゃ）は、[[長崎県]][[対馬市]]美津島町加志に鎮座する[[神社]]である。[[旧県社]]で、かつては加志大明神・賀志宮とも称した。 == 祭神 == 太詔戸神（太祝詞神）と[[雷大臣命]]を祀る。 太詔戸神は[[天児屋命]]の事とされ、雷大臣命は[[津島氏 (神別)|津島直]]の祖神とされる。 == 歴史 == [[806年]]（[[大同 (日本)|大同]]元年）の『[[新抄格勅符抄]]』に「太祝詞神 二戸対馬島」とあり、[[対馬島]]で[[神封戸]]2戸が与えられたとある。 [[927年]]（[[延長 (元号)|延長]]5年）の『[[延喜式神名帳]]』には「貞タ太祝詞神社 名神 大」とあり、[[名神大社]]に列せられている。 == 境内 == 雷大臣命の墓と伝わる壇がある。 == 参考文献 == *『長崎県の地名』（日本歴史地名大系43）、平凡社、2001年ISBN 4-582-49043-3 == 関連項目 == *[[森神社 (天理市)]] - かつて「太祝詞神社」と称した。 *[[祝詞神社]] - 長崎県[[佐世保市]]に鎮座する神社。 {{commonscat|Futonorito-jinja}} {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:ふとのりとしんしや}} [[Category:長崎県の神社]] [[Category:対馬市]] [[Category:名神大社]] [[Category:対馬国の式内社|名ふとのりとしんしや]] [[Category:長崎県の旧県社]]

revision.comment: [[Category:対馬国の式内社]]を除去; [[Category:対馬島の式内社]]を追加 ([[WP:HotCat|HotCat]]使用)

{{出典の明記|date=2018-07-20}} {{神社 |名称 = 太祝詞神社 |画像 = [[ファイル:Hutonorito jinja Haiden.JPG|250px|拝殿]]
拝殿 |所在地 = 長崎県対馬市美津島町加志512 |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|16|44.1|N|129|14|23.8|E|region:JP-42_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=太祝詞神社}} |祭神 = [[太詔戸神]]・[[中臣烏賊津|雷大臣命]] |社格 = 式内社（名神大）・旧県社 |創建 = |本殿 = [[流造]] |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = 陰暦9月9日 |神事 = }} [[ファイル:Futonorito jinja Gate.JPG|thumb|right|250px|鳥居]] [[ファイル:Futonorito jinja Honden.JPG|thumb|right|250px|本殿]] '''太祝詞神社'''（ふとのりとじんじゃ）は、[[長崎県]][[対馬市]]美津島町加志に鎮座する[[神社]]である。[[旧県社]]で、かつては加志大明神・賀志宮とも称した。 == 祭神 == 太詔戸神（太祝詞神）と[[中臣烏賊津|雷大臣命]]を祀る。 == 歴史 == [[806年]]（[[大同 (日本)|大同]]元年）の『[[新抄格勅符抄]]』に「太祝詞神 二戸対馬島」とあり、[[対馬島]]で[[神封戸]]2戸が与えられたとある。 [[927年]]（[[延長 (元号)|延長]]5年）の『[[延喜式神名帳]]』には「貞タ太祝詞神社 名神 大」とあり、[[名神大社]]に列せられている。 == 境内 == 雷大臣命の墓と伝わる壇がある。 == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} == 参考文献 == * 『長崎県の地名』（日本歴史地名大系43）、平凡社、2001年、ISBN 4-582-49043-3 == 関連項目 == * [[森神社 (天理市)]] - かつて「太祝詞神社」と称した。 * [[祝詞神社]] - 長崎県[[佐世保市]]に鎮座する神社。 {{commonscat|Futonorito-jinja}} {{神道 横}} {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:ふとのりとしんしや}} [[Category:長崎県の神社]] [[Category:対馬市の歴史]] [[Category:名神大社]] [[Category:対馬島の式内社|名ふとのりとしんしや]] [[Category:長崎県の旧県社]]

{{Unreferenced|date=July 20, 2018}} {{Infobox Shinto Shrine | Name = Futonorito Shrine | Image = [[File:Hutonorito jinja Haiden.JPG|250px|Haiden]]
Haiden | Location = 512 Kashi, Mitsushima Town, Tsushima City, Nagasaki Prefecture, Japan | Coordinates = {{Coord|34|16|44.1|N|129|14|23.8|E|region:JP-42_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Futonorito Shrine}} | Deity = Futonorito-no-Kami (Great Pronouncement God), [[Nakatomi no Ikatsu|Ikatsu Daijin-no-Mikoto]] | Shrine Rank = Shikinaisha (Myōjin Taisha), Former Prefectural Shrine | Established = Unknown | Honden = [[Nagare-zukuri]] | Alternate Name = Kashi Daimyōjin, Kashi-no-Miya | Pilgrimage = | Annual Festival = 9th day of the 9th lunar month | Rituals = }} [[File:Futonorito jinja Gate.JPG|thumb|right|250px|Torii gate]] [[File:Futonorito jinja Honden.JPG|thumb|right|250px|Honden]] **Futonorito Shrine** (太祝詞神社, *Futonorito Jinja*) is a **Shinto shrine** located in **Kashi, Mitsushima Town, Tsushima City, Nagasaki Prefecture**. It was formerly classified as a **Prefectural Shrine** (*Ken-sha*) and was historically known as **Kashi Daimyōjin** or **Kashi-no-Miya**. ## **Deities Enshrined** The shrine venerates: - **Futonorito-no-Kami** (*太詔戸神, also called 太祝詞神* – "Great Pronouncement God") - **Ikatsu Daijin-no-Mikoto** (*雷大臣命, also known as Nakatomi no Ikatsu*) ## **History** - **806 CE (Daidō 1st year)**: Mentioned in the **Shinshō Kyakuchokufu-shō** (『新抄格勅符抄』) as "Futonorito Shrine, 2 households, Tsushima Island," indicating it was granted **two divine households** (*shinfuko*) for its upkeep. - **927 CE (Engi 5th year)**: Listed in the **Engishiki Jinmyōchō** (『延喜式神名帳』) as **"Teita Futonorito Shrine, Myōjin Taisha"**, indicating its status as a **Myōjin Taisha**, a high-ranking shrine of great divine virtue. ## **Shrine Grounds** Within the precincts, there is a **mound said to be the tomb of Ikatsu Daijin-no-Mikoto**. ## **References** {{Reflist}} ## **Bibliography** - *Nagano Prefecture Place Names* (日本歴史地名大系43), Heibonsha, 2001, ISBN 4-582-49043-3 ## **Related Shrines** - **Mori Shrine (Tenri City)** – Previously known as "Futonorito Shrine." - **Norito Shrine (Sasebo City, Nagasaki Prefecture)** – Another shrine with a similar name. {{Commonscat|Futonorito-jinja}} {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Futonorito Shrine}} [[Category:Shinto Shrines in Nagasaki Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings in Tsushima City]] [[Category:History of Tsushima City]] [[Category:Myōjin Taisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha of Tsushima Island]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Nagasaki]] * [[Nakatomi no Ikatsu]] * {{ill|Tai Norito Shrine|ja|太祝詞神社}} in Mitsushima Town is believed to house his grave. ... 6 KB (818 words) - 23:32, 17 January 2025 [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:Search started pages]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:dd]] [[Category:properly attributed pages]][[Category:Tai Norito Shrine]][[Category:properly attributed pages]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:Tai Norito Shrine]] [[Category:太祝詞神社]][[ja:太祝詞神社]]

It was formerly classified as a **Prefectural Shrine** (*Ken-sha*) and was historically known as **Kashi Daimyōjin** or **Kashi-no-Miya**. ## **Deities Enshrined** The shrine venerates: - **Futonorito-no-Kami** (*太詔戸神, also called 太祝詞神* – "Great Pronouncement God") - **Ikatsu Daijin-no-Mikoto** (*雷大臣命, also known as Nakatomi no Ikatsu*) ## **History** - **806 CE (Daidō 1st year)**: Mentioned in the **Shinshō Kyakuchokufu-shō** (『新抄格勅符抄』) as "Futonorito Shrine, 2 households, Tsushima Island," indicating it was granted **two divine households** (*shinfuko*) for its upkeep. - **927 CE (Engi 5th year)**: Listed in the **Engishiki Jinmyōchō** (『延喜式神名帳』) as **"Teita Futonorito Shrine, Myōjin Taisha"**, indicating its status as a **Myōjin Taisha**, a high-ranking shrine of great divine virtue. ## **Shrine Grounds** Within the precincts, there is a **mound said to be the tomb of Ikatsu Daijin-no-Mikoto**. ## **References** {{Reflist}} ## **Bibliography** - *Nagano Prefecture Place Names* (日本歴史地名大系43), Heibonsha, 2001, ISBN 4-582-49043-3 ## **Related Shrines** - **Mori Shrine (Tenri City)** – Previously known as "Futonorito Shrine." - **Norito Shrine (Sasebo City, Nagasaki Prefecture)** – Another shrine with a similar name. {{Commonscat|Futonorito-jinja}} {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Futonorito Shrine}} [[Category:Shinto Shrines in Nagasaki Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings in Tsushima City]] [[Category:History of Tsushima City]] [[Category:Myōjin Taisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha of Tsushima Island]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Nagasaki]] * [[Nakatomi no Ikatsu]] * {{ill|Tai Norito Shrine|ja|太祝詞神社}} in Mitsushima Town is believed to house his grave. ... 6 KB (818 words) - **927 CE (Engi 5th year)**: Listed in the **Engishiki Jinmyōchō** (『延喜式神名帳』) as **"Teita Futonorito Shrine, Myōjin Taisha"**, indicating its status as a **Myōjin Taisha**, a high-ranking shrine of great divine virtue. ## **Shrine Grounds** Within the precincts, there is a **mound said to be the tomb of Ikatsu Daijin-no-Mikoto**. ## **References** {{Reflist}} ## **Bibliography** - Haiden | Location = 512 Kashi, Mitsushima Town, Tsushima City, Nagasaki Prefecture, Japan | Coordinates = {{Coord|34|16|44.1|N|129|14|23.8|E|region:JP-42_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Futonorito Shrine}} | Deity = Futonorito-no-Kami (Great Pronouncement God), [[Nakatomi no Ikatsu|Ikatsu Daijin-no-Mikoto]] | Shrine Rank = Shikinaisha (Myōjin Taisha), Former Prefectural Shrine | Established = Unknown | Honden = [[Nagare-zukuri]] | Alternate Name = Kashi Daimyōjin, Kashi-no-Miya | Pilgrimage = | Annual Festival = 9th day of the 9th lunar month | Rituals = }} [[File:Futonorito jinja Gate.JPG|thumb|right|250px|Torii gate]] [[File:Futonorito jinja Honden.JPG|thumb|right|250px|Honden]] **Futonorito Shrine** (太祝詞神社, *Futonorito Jinja*) is a **Shinto shrine** located in **Kashi, Mitsushima Town, Tsushima City, Nagasaki Prefecture**. It was formerly classified as a **Prefectural Shrine** (*Ken-sha*) and was historically known as **Kashi Daimyōjin** or **Kashi-no-Miya**. ## **Deities Enshrined** The shrine venerates: - **Futonorito-no-Kami** (*太詔戸神, also called 太祝詞神* – "Great Pronouncement God") - **Ikatsu Daijin-no-Mikoto** (*雷大臣命, also known as Nakatomi no Ikatsu*) ## **History** - **806 CE (Daidō 1st year)**: Mentioned in the **Shinshō Kyakuchokufu-shō** (『新抄格勅符抄』) as "Futonorito Shrine, 2 households, Tsushima Island," indicating it was granted **two divine households** (*shinfuko*) for its upkeep. - **927 CE (Engi 5th year)**: Listed in the **Engishiki Jinmyōchō** (『延喜式神名帳』) as **"Teita Futonorito Shrine, Myōjin Taisha"**, indicating its status as a **Myōjin Taisha**, a high-ranking shrine of great divine virtue. ## **Shrine Grounds** Within the precincts, there is a **mound said to be the tomb of Ikatsu Daijin-no-Mikoto**. ## **References** {{Reflist}} ## **Bibliography** - *Nagano Prefecture Place Names* (日本歴史地名大系43), Heibonsha, 2001, ISBN 4-582-49043-3 ## **Related Shrines** - **Mori Shrine (Tenri City)** – Previously known as "Futonorito Shrine." - **Norito Shrine (Sasebo City, Nagasaki Prefecture)** – Another shrine with a similar name. {{Commonscat|Futonorito-jinja}} {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Futonorito Shrine}} [[Category:Shinto Shrines in Nagasaki Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings in Tsushima City]] [[Category:History of Tsushima City]] [[Category:Myōjin Taisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha of Tsushima Island]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Nagasaki]] * [[Nakatomi no Ikatsu]] * {{ill|Tai Norito Shrine|ja|太祝詞神社}} in Mitsushima Town is believed to house his grave. ... 6 KB (818 words) - Haiden | Location = 512 Kashi, Mitsushima Town, Tsushima City, Nagasaki Prefecture, Japan | Coordinates = {{Coord|34|16|44.1|N|129|14|23.8|E|region:JP-42_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Futonorito Shrine}} | Deity = Futonorito-no-Kami (Great Pronouncement God), [[Nakatomi no Ikatsu|Ikatsu Daijin-no-Mikoto]] | Shrine Rank = Shikinaisha (Myōjin Taisha), Former Prefectural Shrine | Established = Unknown | Honden = [[Nagare-zukuri]] | Alternate Name = Kashi Daimyōjin, Kashi-no-Miya | Pilgrimage = | Annual Festival = 9th day of the 9th lunar month | Rituals = }} [[File:Futonorito jinja Gate.JPG|thumb|right|250px|Torii gate]] [[File:Futonorito jinja Honden.JPG|thumb|right|250px|Honden]] **Futonorito Shrine** (太祝詞神社, *Futonorito Jinja*) is a **Shinto shrine** located in **Kashi, Mitsushima Town, Tsushima City, Nagasaki Prefecture**. It was formerly classified as a **Prefectural Shrine** (*Ken-sha*) and was historically known as **Kashi Daimyōjin** or **Kashi-no-Miya**. ## **Deities Enshrined** The shrine venerates: - **Futonorito-no-Kami** (*太詔戸神, also called 太祝詞神* – "Great Pronouncement God") - **Ikatsu Daijin-no-Mikoto** (*雷大臣命, also known as Nakatomi no Ikatsu*) ## **History** - **806 CE (Daidō 1st year)**: Mentioned in the **Shinshō Kyakuchokufu-shō** (『新抄格勅符抄』) as "Futonorito Shrine, 2 households, Tsushima Island," indicating it was granted **two divine households** (*shinfuko*) for its upkeep. - **927 CE (Engi 5th year)**: Listed in the **Engishiki Jinmyōchō** (『延喜式神名帳』) as **"Teita Futonorito Shrine, Myōjin Taisha"**, indicating its status as a **Myōjin Taisha**, a high-ranking shrine of great divine virtue. ## **Shrine Grounds** Within the precincts, there is a **mound said to be the tomb of Ikatsu Daijin-no-Mikoto**. ## **References** {{Reflist}} ## **Bibliography** - *Nagano Prefecture Place Names* (日本歴史地名大系43), Heibonsha, 2001, ISBN 4-582-49043-3 ## **Related Shrines** - **Mori Shrine (Tenri City)** – Previously known as "Futonorito Shrine." - **Norito Shrine (Sasebo City, Nagasaki Prefecture)** – Another shrine with a similar name. {{Commonscat|Futonorito-jinja}} {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Futonorito Shrine}} [[Category:Shinto Shrines in Nagasaki Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings in Tsushima City]] [[Category:History of Tsushima City]] [[Category:Myōjin Taisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha of Tsushima Island]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Nagasaki]] * [[Nakatomi no Ikatsu]] * {{ill|Tai Norito Shrine|ja|太祝詞神社}} in Mitsushima Town is believed to house his grave. ... 6 KB (818 words) - It was formerly classified as a '''Prefectural Shrine''' (*Ken-sha*) and was historically known as '''Kashi Daimyōjin''' or '''Kashi-no-Miya'''. == '''Deities Enshrined''' == The shrine venerates: - '''Futonorito-no-Kami''' (*太詔戸神, also called 太祝詞神* – "Great Pronouncement God") - '''Ikatsu Daijin-no-Mikoto''' (*雷大臣命, also known as Nakatomi no Ikatsu*) == '''History''' == - '''806 CE (Daidō 1st year)''': Mentioned in the '''Shinshō Kyakuchokufu-shō''' (『新抄格勅符抄』) as "Futonorito Shrine, 2 households, Tsushima Island," indicating it was granted '''two divine households''' (*shinfuko*) for its upkeep. - '''927 CE (Engi 5th year)''': Listed in the '''Engishiki Jinmyōchō''' (『延喜式神名帳』) as '''"Teita Futonorito Shrine, Myōjin Taisha"''', indicating its status as a '''Myōjin Taisha''', a high-ranking shrine of great divine virtue. == '''Shrine Grounds''' == Within the precincts, there is a '''mound said to be the tomb of Ikatsu Daijin-no-Mikoto'''. == '''References''' == {{Reflist}} == '''Bibliography''' == - *Nagano Prefecture Place Names* (日本歴史地名大系43), Heibonsha, 2001, ISBN 4-582-49043-3 == '''Related Shrines''' == - '''Mori Shrine (Tenri City)''' – Previously known as "Futonorito Shrine." - '''Norito Shrine (Sasebo City, Nagasaki Prefecture)''' – Another shrine with a similar name. {{Commonscat|Futonorito-jinja}} {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Futonorito Shrine}} [[Category:Shinto Shrines in Nagasaki Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings in Tsushima City]] [[Category:History of Tsushima City]] [[Category:Myōjin Taisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha of Tsushima Island]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Nagasaki]] * [[Nakatomi no Ikatsu]] * {{ill|Tai Norito Shrine|ja|太祝詞神社}} in Mitsushima Town is believed to house his grave. ... 6 KB (818 words) - Haiden | Location = 512 Kashi, Mitsushima Town, Tsushima City, Nagasaki Prefecture, Japan | Coordinates = {{Coord|34|16|44.1|N|129|14|23.8|E|region:JP-42_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Futonorito Shrine}} | Deity = Futonorito-no-Kami (Great Pronouncement God), [[Nakatomi no Ikatsu|Ikatsu Daijin-no-Mikoto]] | Shrine Rank = Shikinaisha (Myōjin Taisha), Former Prefectural Shrine | Established = Unknown | Honden = [[Nagare-zukuri]] | Alternate Name = Kashi Daimyōjin, Kashi-no-Miya | Pilgrimage = | Annual Festival = 9th day of the 9th lunar month | Rituals = }} [[File:Futonorito jinja Gate.JPG|thumb|right|250px|Torii gate]] [[File:Futonorito jinja Honden.JPG|thumb|right|250px|Honden]] '''Futonorito Shrine''' (太祝詞神社, *Futonorito Jinja*) is a '''Shinto shrine''' located in '''Kashi, Mitsushima Town, Tsushima City, Nagasaki Prefecture'''. It was formerly classified as a '''Prefectural Shrine''' (*Ken-sha*) and was historically known as '''Kashi Daimyōjin''' or '''Kashi-no-Miya'''. == '''Deities Enshrined''' == The shrine venerates: * '''Futonorito-no-Kami''' (*太詔戸神, also called 太祝詞神* – "Great Pronouncement God") * '''Ikatsu Daijin-no-Mikoto''' (*雷大臣命, also known as Nakatomi no Ikatsu*) == '''History''' == * '''806 CE (Daidō 1st year)''': Mentioned in the '''Shinshō Kyakuchokufu-shō''' (『新抄格勅符抄』) as "Futonorito Shrine, 2 households, Tsushima Island," indicating it was granted '''two divine households''' (*shinfuko*) for its upkeep. * '''927 CE (Engi 5th year)''': Listed in the '''Engishiki Jinmyōchō''' (『延喜式神名帳』) as '''"Teita Futonorito Shrine, Myōjin Taisha"''', indicating its status as a '''Myōjin Taisha''', a high-ranking shrine of great divine virtue. == '''Shrine Grounds''' == Within the precincts, there is a '''mound said to be the tomb of Ikatsu Daijin-no-Mikoto'''. == '''References''' == {{Reflist}} == '''Bibliography''' == - *Nagano Prefecture Place Names* (日本歴史地名大系43), Heibonsha, 2001, ISBN 4-582-49043-3 == '''Related Shrines''' == * '''Mori Shrine (Tenri City)''' – Previously known as "Futonorito Shrine." * '''Norito Shrine (Sasebo City, Nagasaki Prefecture)''' – Another shrine with a similar name. {{Commonscat|Futonorito-jinja}} {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Futonorito Shrine}} [[Category:Shinto Shrines in Nagasaki Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings in Tsushima City]] [[Category:History of Tsushima City]] [[Category:Myōjin Taisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha of Tsushima Island]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Nagasaki]] * [[Nakatomi no Ikatsu]] * {{ill|Tai Norito Shrine|ja|太祝詞神社}} in Mitsushima Town is believed to house his grave. ... 6 KB (818 words) - Haiden | Location = 512 Kashi, Mitsushima Town, Tsushima City, Nagasaki Prefecture, Japan | Coordinates = {{Coord|34|16|44.1|N|129|14|23.8|E|region:JP-42_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Futonorito Shrine}} | Deity = Futonorito-no-Kami (Great Pronouncement God), [[Nakatomi no Ikatsu|Ikatsu Daijin-no-Mikoto]] | Shrine Rank = Shikinaisha (Myōjin Taisha), Former Prefectural Shrine | Established = Unknown | Honden = [[Nagare-zukuri]] | Alternate Name = Kashi Daimyōjin, Kashi-no-Miya | Pilgrimage = | Annual Festival = 9th day of the 9th lunar month | Rituals = }} [[File:Futonorito jinja Gate.JPG|thumb|right|250px|Torii gate]] [[File:Futonorito jinja Honden.JPG|thumb|right|250px|Honden]] '''Futonorito Shrine''' (太祝詞神社, *Futonorito Jinja*) is a '''Shinto shrine''' located in '''Kashi, Mitsushima Town, Tsushima City, Nagasaki Prefecture'''. It was formerly classified as a '''Prefectural Shrine''' (*Ken-sha*) and was historically known as '''Kashi Daimyōjin''' or '''Kashi-no-Miya'''. == '''Deities Enshrined''' == The shrine venerates: * '''Futonorito-no-Kami''' (*太詔戸神, also called 太祝詞神* – "Great Pronouncement God") * '''Ikatsu Daijin-no-Mikoto''' (*雷大臣命, also known as Nakatomi no Ikatsu*) == '''History''' == * '''806 CE (Daidō 1st year)''': Mentioned in the '''Shinshō Kyakuchokufu-shō''' (『新抄格勅符抄』) as "Futonorito Shrine, 2 households, Tsushima Island," indicating it was granted '''two divine households''' (*shinfuko*) for its upkeep. * '''927 CE (Engi 5th year)''': Listed in the '''Engishiki Jinmyōchō''' (『延喜式神名帳』) as '''"Teita Futonorito Shrine, Myōjin Taisha"''', indicating its status as a '''Myōjin Taisha''', a high-ranking shrine of great divine virtue. == '''Shrine Grounds''' == Within the precincts, there is a '''mound said to be the tomb of Ikatsu Daijin-no-Mikoto'''. == '''References''' == {{Reflist}} == '''Bibliography''' == - *Nagano Prefecture Place Names* (日本歴史地名大系43), Heibonsha, 2001, ISBN 4-582-49043-3 == '''Related Shrines''' == * '''Mori Shrine (Tenri City)''' – Previously known as "Futonorito Shrine." * '''Norito Shrine (Sasebo City, Nagasaki Prefecture)''' – Another shrine with a similar name. {{Commonscat|Futonorito-jinja}} {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{Shinto-stub}} * [[Nakatomi no Ikatsu]] * {{ill|Tai Norito Shrine|ja|太祝詞神社|12=draft}} in Mitsushima Town is believed to house his grave. ... 6 KB (818 words) - {{Unreferenced|date=July 20, 2018}} {{Infobox Shinto Shrine | Name = Futonorito Shrine | image = [[File:Hutonorito jinja Haiden.JPG|250px|Haiden]]
Haiden | Location = 512 Kashi, Mitsushima Town, Tsushima City, Nagasaki Prefecture, Japan | Coordinates = {{Coord|34|16|44.1|N|129|14|23.8|E|region:JP-42_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Futonorito Shrine}} | Deity = Futonorito-no-Kami (Great Pronouncement God), [[Nakatomi no Ikatsu|Ikatsu Daijin-no-Mikoto]] | Shrine Rank = Shikinaisha (Myōjin Taisha), Former Prefectural Shrine | Established = Unknown | Honden = [[Nagare-zukuri]] | Alternate Name = Kashi Daimyōjin, Kashi-no-Miya | Pilgrimage = | Annual Festival = 9th day of the 9th lunar month | Rituals = }} [[File:Futonorito jinja Gate.JPG|thumb|right|250px|Torii gate]] [[File:Futonorito jinja Honden.JPG|thumb|right|250px|Honden]] '''Futonorito Shrine''' (太祝詞神社, *Futonorito Jinja*) is a '''Shinto shrine''' located in '''Kashi, Mitsushima Town, Tsushima City, Nagasaki Prefecture'''. It was formerly classified as a '''Prefectural Shrine''' (*Ken-sha*) and was historically known as '''Kashi Daimyōjin''' or '''Kashi-no-Miya'''. == '''Deities Enshrined''' == The shrine venerates: * '''Futonorito-no-Kami''' (*太詔戸神, also called 太祝詞神* – "Great Pronouncement God") * '''Ikatsu Daijin-no-Mikoto''' (*雷大臣命, also known as Nakatomi no Ikatsu*) == '''History''' == * '''806 CE (Daidō 1st year)''': Mentioned in the '''Shinshō Kyakuchokufu-shō''' (『新抄格勅符抄』) as "Futonorito Shrine, 2 households, Tsushima Island," indicating it was granted '''two divine households''' (*shinfuko*) for its upkeep. * '''927 CE (Engi 5th year)''': Listed in the '''Engishiki Jinmyōchō''' (『延喜式神名帳』) as '''"Teita Futonorito Shrine, Myōjin Taisha"''', indicating its status as a '''Myōjin Taisha''', a high-Ranking shrine of great divine virtue. == '''Shrine Grounds''' == Within the precincts, there is a '''mound said to be the tomb of Ikatsu Daijin-no-Mikoto'''. == '''References''' == {{Reflist}} == '''Bibliography''' == - *Nagano Prefecture Place Names* (日本歴史地名大系43), Heibonsha, 2001, ISBN 4-582-49043-3 == '''Related Shrines''' == * '''Mori Shrine (Tenri City)''' – Previously known as "Futonorito Shrine." * '''Norito Shrine (Sasebo City, Nagasaki Prefecture)''' – Another shrine with a similar name. {{Commonscat|Futonorito-jinja}} {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{Shinto-stub}} * [[Nakatomi no Ikatsu]] * {{ill|Tai Norito Shrine|ja|太祝詞神社|12=draft|12=draft}} in Mitsushima Town is believed to house his grave. ... 6 KB (818 words) - 23:32, 17 January 2025 {{draft categories| }} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto Shrines in Nagasaki Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings in Tsushima City]] [[Category:History of Tsushima City]] [[Category:Myōjin Taisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha of Tsushima Island]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Nagasaki]] [[Category:Completion tier 7]] [[Category:qq]]{{citation needed|Tai Norito Shrine}}[[Category:qq]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Search started pages]] [[Category:Tai Norito Shrine]] [[Category:太祝詞神社]] }} [[sn:Tai Norito Shrine]] [[ja:太祝詞神社]] [[sn:ai Norito Shrine]] {{citation needed|Futonorito Shrine}} [[Category:Ambox transclusions]]

{{Unreferenced|date=July 20, 2018}} {{Infobox Shinto Shrine | Name = Futonorito Shrine | image = [[File:Hutonorito jinja Haiden.JPG|250px|Haiden]]
Haiden | Location = 512 Kashi, Mitsushima Town, Tsushima City, Nagasaki Prefecture, Japan | Coordinates = {{Coord|34|16|44.1|N|129|14|23.8|E|region:JP-42_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Futonorito Shrine}} | Deity = Futonorito-no-Kami (Great Pronouncement God), [[Nakatomi no Ikatsu|Ikatsu Daijin-no-Mikoto]] | Shrine Rank = Shikinaisha (Myōjin Taisha), Former Prefectural Shrine | Established = Unknown | Honden = [[Nagare-zukuri]] | Alternate Name = Kashi Daimyōjin, Kashi-no-Miya | Pilgrimage = | Annual Festival = 9th day of the 9th lunar month | Rituals = }} [[File:Futonorito jinja Gate.JPG|thumb|right|250px|Torii gate]] [[File:Futonorito jinja Honden.JPG|thumb|right|250px|Honden]] '''Futonorito Shrine''' (太祝詞神社, *Futonorito Jinja*) is a '''Shinto shrine''' located in '''Kashi, Mitsushima Town, Tsushima City, Nagasaki Prefecture'''. It was formerly classified as a '''Prefectural Shrine''' (*Ken-sha*) and was historically known as '''Kashi Daimyōjin''' or '''Kashi-no-Miya'''. == '''Deities Enshrined''' == The shrine venerates: * '''Futonorito-no-Kami''' (*太詔戸神, also called 太祝詞神* – "Great Pronouncement God") * '''Ikatsu Daijin-no-Mikoto''' (*雷大臣命, also known as Nakatomi no Ikatsu*) == '''History''' == * '''806 CE (Daidō 1st year)''': Mentioned in the '''Shinshō Kyakuchokufu-shō''' (『新抄格勅符抄』) as "Futonorito Shrine, 2 households, Tsushima Island," indicating it was granted '''two divine households''' (*shinfuko*) for its upkeep. * '''927 CE (Engi 5th year)''': Listed in the '''Engishiki Jinmyōchō''' (『延喜式神名帳』) as '''"Teita Futonorito Shrine, Myōjin Taisha"''', indicating its status as a '''Myōjin Taisha''', a high-Ranking shrine of great divine virtue. == '''Shrine Grounds''' == Within the precincts, there is a '''mound said to be the tomb of Ikatsu Daijin-no-Mikoto'''. == '''References''' == {{Reflist}} == '''Bibliography''' == - *Nagano Prefecture Place Names* (日本歴史地名大系43), Heibonsha, 2001, ISBN 4-582-49043-3 == '''Related Shrines''' == * '''Mori Shrine (Tenri City)''' – Previously known as "Futonorito Shrine." * '''Norito Shrine (Sasebo City, Nagasaki Prefecture)''' – Another shrine with a similar name. {{Commonscat|Futonorito-jinja}} {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{Shinto-stub}} * [[Nakatomi no Ikatsu]] * {{ill|Tai Norito Shrine|ja|太祝詞神社|12=draft|12=draft}} in Mitsushima Town is believed to house his grave. ... 6 KB (818 words) - Haiden | Location = 512 Kashi, Mitsushima Town, Tsushima City, Nagasaki Prefecture, Japan | Coordinates = {{Coord|34|16|44.1|N|129|14|23.8|E|region:JP-42_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Futonorito Shrine}} | Deity = Futonorito-no-Kami (Great Pronouncement God), [[Nakatomi no Ikatsu|Ikatsu Daijin-no-Mikoto]] | Shrine Rank = Shikinaisha (Myōjin Taisha), Former Prefectural Shrine | Established = Unknown | Honden = [[Nagare-zukuri]] | Alternate Name = Kashi Daimyōjin, Kashi-no-Miya | Pilgrimage = | Annual Festival = 9th day of the 9th lunar month | Rituals = }} [[File:Futonorito jinja Gate.JPG|thumb|right|250px|Torii gate]] [[File:Futonorito jinja Honden.JPG|thumb|right|250px|Honden]] '''Futonorito Shrine''' (太祝詞神社, *Futonorito Jinja*) is a '''Shinto shrine''' located in '''Kashi, Mitsushima Town, Tsushima City, Nagasaki Prefecture'''. It was formerly classified as a '''Prefectural Shrine''' (*Ken-sha*) and was historically known as '''Kashi Daimyōjin''' or '''Kashi-no-Miya'''. == '''Deities Enshrined''' == The shrine venerates: * '''Futonorito-no-Kami''' (*太詔戸神, also called 太祝詞神* – "Great Pronouncement God") * '''Ikatsu Daijin-no-Mikoto''' (*雷大臣命, also known as Nakatomi no Ikatsu*) == '''History''' == * '''806 CE (Daidō 1st year)''': Mentioned in the '''Shinshō Kyakuchokufu-shō''' (『新抄格勅符抄』) as "Futonorito Shrine, 2 households, Tsushima Island," indicating it was granted '''two divine households''' (*shinfuko*) for its upkeep. * '''927 CE (Engi 5th year)''': Listed in the '''Engishiki Jinmyōchō''' (『延喜式神名帳』) as '''"Teita Futonorito Shrine, Myōjin Taisha"''', indicating its status as a '''Myōjin Taisha''', a high-Ranking shrine of great divine virtue. == '''Shrine Grounds''' == Within the precincts, there is a '''mound said to be the tomb of Ikatsu Daijin-no-Mikoto'''. == '''References''' == {{Reflist}} == '''Bibliography''' == - *Nagano Prefecture Place Names* (日本歴史地名大系43), Heibonsha, 2001, ISBN 4-582-49043-3 == '''Related Shrines''' == * '''Mori Shrine (Tenri City)''' – Previously known as "Futonorito Shrine." * '''Norito Shrine (Sasebo City, Nagasaki Prefecture)''' – Another shrine with a similar name. {{Commonscat|Futonorito-jinja}} {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{Shinto-stub}} * [[Nakatomi no Ikatsu]] * {{ill|Tai Norito Shrine|ja|太祝詞神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} in Mitsushima Town is believed to house his grave. ... 6 KB (818 words) - 23:32, 17 January 2025 }} {{translated page|ja|太祝詞神社|version=92572230|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto Shrines in Nagasaki Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings in Tsushima City]] [[Category:History of Tsushima City]] [[Category:Myōjin Taisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Tsushima Island]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Nagasaki]] [[Category:Completion tier 7]] [[sn:Tai Norito Shrine]] [[ja:太祝詞神社]] [[sn:ai Norito Shrine]]

{{Unreferenced|date=July 20, 2018}} {{Infobox Shinto Shrine | Name = Futonorito Shrine | image = [[File:Hutonorito jinja Haiden.JPG|250px|Haiden]]
Haiden | Location = 512 Kashi, Mitsushima Town, Tsushima City, Nagasaki Prefecture, Japan | Coordinates = {{Coord|34|16|44.1|N|129|14|23.8|E|region:JP-42_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Futonorito Shrine}} | Deity = Futonorito-no-Kami (Great Pronouncement God), [[Nakatomi no Ikatsu|Ikatsu Daijin-no-Mikoto]] | Shrine Rank = Shikinaisha (Myōjin Taisha), Former Prefectural Shrine | Established = Unknown | Honden = [[Nagare-zukuri]] | Alternate Name = Kashi Daimyōjin, Kashi-no-Miya | Pilgrimage = | Annual Festival = 9th day of the 9th lunar month | Rituals = }} [[File:Futonorito jinja Gate.JPG|thumb|right|250px|Torii gate]] [[File:Futonorito jinja Honden.JPG|thumb|right|250px|Honden]] '''Futonorito Shrine''' (太祝詞神社, *Futonorito Jinja*) is a '''Shinto shrine''' located in '''Kashi, Mitsushima Town, Tsushima City, Nagasaki Prefecture'''. It was formerly classified as a '''Prefectural Shrine''' (*Ken-sha*) and was historically known as '''Kashi Daimyōjin''' or '''Kashi-no-Miya'''. == '''Deities Enshrined''' == The shrine venerates: * '''Futonorito-no-Kami''' (*太詔戸神, also called 太祝詞神* – "Great Pronouncement God") * '''Ikatsu Daijin-no-Mikoto''' (*雷大臣命, also known as Nakatomi no Ikatsu*) == '''History''' == * '''806 CE (Daidō 1st year)''': Mentioned in the '''Shinshō Kyakuchokufu-shō''' (『新抄格勅符抄』) as "Futonorito Shrine, 2 households, Tsushima Island," indicating it was granted '''two divine households''' (*shinfuko*) for its upkeep. * '''927 CE (Engi 5th year)''': Listed in the '''Engishiki Jinmyōchō''' (『延喜式神名帳』) as '''"Teita Futonorito Shrine, Myōjin Taisha"''', indicating its status as a '''Myōjin Taisha''', a high-Ranking shrine of great divine virtue. == '''Shrine Grounds''' == Within the precincts, there is a '''mound said to be the tomb of Ikatsu Daijin-no-Mikoto'''. == '''References''' == {{Reflist}} == '''Bibliography''' == - *Nagano Prefecture Place Names* (日本歴史地名大系43), Heibonsha, 2001, ISBN 4-582-49043-3 == '''Related Shrines''' == * '''Mori Shrine (Tenri City)''' – Previously known as "Futonorito Shrine." * '''Norito Shrine (Sasebo City, Nagasaki Prefecture)''' – Another shrine with a similar name. {{Commonscat|Futonorito-jinja}} {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{Shinto-stub}} * [[Nakatomi no Ikatsu]] * {{ill|Tai Norito Shrine|ja|太祝詞神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tai Norito Shrine|qq=}} in Mitsushima Town is believed to house his grave. ... 6 KB (818 words) - 23:32, 17 January 2025 }} {{translated page|ja|太祝詞神社|version=92572230|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto Shrines in Nagasaki Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings in Tsushima City]] [[Category:History of Tsushima City]] [[Category:Myōjin Taisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Tsushima Island]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Nagasaki Prefecture]] [[Category:Completion tier 7]] [[sn:Tai Norito Shrine]] [[ja:太祝詞神社]] [[sn:ai Norito Shrine]] [[Category:Buildings in Tsushima, Nagasaki]] [[Category:History of Tsushima, Nagasaki]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nagasaki Prefecture]]

{{Unreferenced|date=July 20, 2018}} {{Infobox Shinto Shrine | Name = Futonorito Shrine | image = [[File:Hutonorito jinja Haiden.JPG|250px|Haiden]]
Haiden | Location = 512 Kashi, Mitsushima Town, Tsushima City, Nagasaki Prefecture, Japan | Coordinates = {{Coord|34|16|44.1|N|129|14|23.8|E|region:JP-42_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Futonorito Shrine}} | Deity = Futonorito-no-Kami (Great Pronouncement God), [[Nakatomi no Ikatsu|Ikatsu Daijin-no-Mikoto]] | Shrine Rank = Shikinaisha (Myōjin Taisha), Former Prefectural Shrine | Established = Unknown | Honden = [[Nagare-zukuri]] | Alternate Name = Kashi Daimyōjin, Kashi-no-Miya | Pilgrimage = | Annual Festival = 9th day of the 9th lunar month | Rituals = }} [[File:Futonorito jinja Gate.JPG|thumb|right|250px|Torii gate]] [[File:Futonorito jinja Honden.JPG|thumb|right|250px|Honden]] '''Futonorito Shrine''' (太祝詞神社, *Futonorito Jinja*) is a '''Shinto shrine''' located in '''Kashi, Mitsushima Town, Tsushima City, Nagasaki Prefecture'''. It was formerly classified as a '''Prefectural Shrine''' (*Ken-sha*) and was historically known as '''Kashi Daimyōjin''' or '''Kashi-no-Miya'''. == '''Deities Enshrined''' == The shrine venerates: * '''Futonorito-no-Kami''' (*太詔戸神, also called 太祝詞神* – "Great Pronouncement God") * '''Ikatsu Daijin-no-Mikoto''' (*雷大臣命, also known as Nakatomi no Ikatsu*) == '''History''' == * '''806 CE (Daidō 1st year)''': Mentioned in the '''Shinshō Kyakuchokufu-shō''' (『新抄格勅符抄』) as "Futonorito Shrine, 2 households, Tsushima Island," indicating it was granted '''two divine households''' (*shinfuko*) for its upkeep. * '''927 CE (Engi 5th year)''': Listed in the '''Engishiki Jinmyōchō''' (『延喜式神名帳』) as '''"Teita Futonorito Shrine, Myōjin Taisha"''', indicating its status as a '''Myōjin Taisha''', a high-Ranking shrine of great divine virtue. == '''Shrine Grounds''' == Within the precincts, there is a '''mound said to be the tomb of Ikatsu Daijin-no-Mikoto'''. == '''References''' == {{Reflist}} == '''Bibliography''' == - *Nagano Prefecture Place Names* (日本歴史地名大系43), Heibonsha, 2001, ISBN 4-582-49043-3 == '''Related Shrines''' == * '''Mori Shrine (Tenri City)''' – Previously known as "Futonorito Shrine." * '''Norito Shrine (Sasebo City, Nagasaki Prefecture)''' – Another shrine with a similar name. {{Commonscat|Futonorito-jinja}} {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{Shinto-stub}} * [[Nakatomi no Ikatsu]] * {{ill|Tai Norito Shrine|ja|太祝詞神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tai Norito Shrine|qq=}} in Mitsushima Town is believed to house his grave. ... 6 KB (818 words) - It was formerly classified as a '''Prefectural Shrine''' (*Ken-sha*) and was historically known as '''Kashi Daimyōjin''' or '''Kashi-no-Miya'''. == '''Deities Enshrined''' == The shrine venerates: * '''Futonorito-no-Kami''' (*太詔戸神, also called 太祝詞神* – "Great Pronouncement God") * '''Ikatsu Daijin-no-Mikoto''' (*雷大臣命, also known as Nakatomi no Ikatsu*) == '''History''' == * '''806 CE (Daidō 1st year)''': Mentioned in the '''Shinshō Kyakuchokufu-shō''' (『新抄格勅符抄』) as "Futonorito Shrine, 2 households, Tsushima Island," indicating it was granted '''two divine households''' (*shinfuko*) for its upkeep. * '''927 CE (Engi 5th year)''': Listed in the '''Engishiki Jinmyōchō''' (『延喜式神名帳』) as '''"Teita Futonorito Shrine, Myōjin Taisha"''', indicating its status as a '''Myōjin Taisha''', a high-Ranking shrine of great divine virtue. == '''Shrine Grounds''' == Within the precincts, there is a '''mound said to be the tomb of Ikatsu Daijin-no-Mikoto'''. == '''References''' == {{Reflist}} == '''Bibliography''' == - *Nagano Prefecture Place Names* (日本歴史地名大系43), Heibonsha, 2001, ISBN 4-582-49043-3 == '''Related Shrines''' == * '''Mori Shrine (Tenri City)''' – Previously known as "Futonorito Shrine." * '''Norito Shrine (Sasebo City, Nagasaki Prefecture)''' – Another shrine with a similar name. {{Commonscat|Futonorito-jinja}} {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{Shinto-stub}} * [[Nakatomi no Ikatsu]] * {{ill|Tai Norito Shrine|ja|太祝詞神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tai Norito Shrine|qq=}} in Mitsushima Town is believed to house his grave. ... 6 KB (818 words) - Haiden | Location = 512 Kashi, Mitsushima Town, Tsushima City, Nagasaki Prefecture, Japan | Coordinates = {{Coord|34|16|44.1|N|129|14|23.8|E|region:JP-42_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Futonorito Shrine}} | Deity = Futonorito-no-Kami (Great Pronouncement God), [[Nakatomi no Ikatsu|Ikatsu Daijin-no-Mikoto]] | Shrine Rank = Shikinaisha (Myōjin Taisha), Former Prefectural Shrine | Established = Unknown | Honden = [[Nagare-zukuri]] | Alternate Name = Kashi Daimyōjin, Kashi-no-Miya | Pilgrimage = | Annual Festival = 9th day of the 9th lunar month | Rituals = }} [[File:Futonorito jinja Gate.JPG|thumb|right|250px|Torii gate]] [[File:Futonorito jinja Honden.JPG|thumb|right|250px|Honden]] '''Futonorito Shrine''' (太祝詞神社, *Futonorito Jinja*) is a '''Shinto shrine''' located in '''Kashi, Mitsushima Town, Tsushima City, Nagasaki Prefecture'''. It was formerly classified as a '''Prefectural Shrine''' (*Ken-sha*) and was historically known as '''Kashi Daimyōjin''' or '''Kashi-no-Miya'''. == '''Deities Enshrined''' == The shrine venerates: * '''Futonorito-no-Kami''' (*太詔戸神, also called 太祝詞神* – "Great Pronouncement God") * '''Ikatsu Daijin-no-Mikoto''' (*雷大臣命, also known as Nakatomi no Ikatsu*) == '''History''' == * '''806 CE (Daidō 1st year)''': Mentioned in the '''Shinshō Kyakuchokufu-shō''' (『新抄格勅符抄』) as "Futonorito Shrine, 2 households, Tsushima Island," indicating it was granted '''two divine households''' (*shinfuko*) for its upkeep. * '''927 CE (Engi 5th year)''': Listed in the '''Engishiki Jinmyōchō''' (『延喜式神名帳』) as '''"Teita Futonorito Shrine, Myōjin Taisha"''', indicating its status as a '''Myōjin Taisha''', a high-Ranking shrine of great divine virtue. == '''Shrine Grounds''' == Within the precincts, there is a '''mound said to be the tomb of Ikatsu Daijin-no-Mikoto'''. == '''References''' == {{Reflist}} == '''Bibliography''' == - *Nagano Prefecture Place Names* (日本歴史地名大系43), Heibonsha, 2001, ISBN 4-582-49043-3 == '''Related Shrines''' == * '''Mori Shrine (Tenri City)''' – Previously known as "Futonorito Shrine." * '''Norito Shrine (Sasebo City, Nagasaki Prefecture)''' – Another shrine with a similar name. {{Commonscat|Futonorito-jinja}} {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{Shinto-stub}} * [[Nakatomi no Ikatsu]] * {{ill|Tai Norito Shrine|ja|太祝詞神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tai Norito Shrine|qq=}} in Mitsushima Town is believed to house his grave. ... 6 KB (818 words) - [[sn:commons:Category:Futonorito-jinja]][[ja:太祝詞神社]] {{Infobox religious building|image=File:Hutonorito jinja Haiden.JPG|prefecture={{ill|Tsushima|WD=Q991032}}|coordinates={{Coord|34.278917|N|129.239944|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}|architecture_style={{ill|nagare-zukuri|WD=Q210684}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11444481]] Wikidata name: Futonorito Shrine [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:41, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Unreferenced|date=July 20, 2018}} {{Infobox Shinto Shrine | Name = Futonorito Shrine | image = [[File:Hutonorito jinja Haiden.JPG|250px|Haiden]]
Haiden | Location = 512 Kashi, Mitsushima Town, Tsushima City, Nagasaki Prefecture, Japan | Coordinates = {{Coord|34|16|44.1|N|129|14|23.8|E|region:JP-42_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Futonorito Shrine}} | Deity = Futonorito-no-Kami (Great Pronouncement God), [[Nakatomi no Ikatsu|Ikatsu Daijin-no-Mikoto]] | Shrine Rank = Shikinaisha (Myōjin Taisha), Former Prefectural Shrine | Established = Unknown | Honden = [[Nagare-zukuri]] | Alternate Name = Kashi Daimyōjin, Kashi-no-Miya | Pilgrimage = | Annual Festival = 9th day of the 9th lunar month | Rituals = }} [[File:Futonorito jinja Gate.JPG|thumb|right|250px|Torii gate]] [[File:Futonorito jinja Honden.JPG|thumb|right|250px|Honden]] '''Futonorito Shrine''' (太祝詞神社, *Futonorito Jinja*) is a '''Shinto shrine''' located in '''Kashi, Mitsushima Town, Tsushima City, Nagasaki Prefecture'''. It was formerly classified as a '''Prefectural Shrine''' (*Ken-sha*) and was historically known as '''Kashi Daimyōjin''' or '''Kashi-no-Miya'''. == '''Deities Enshrined''' == The shrine venerates: * '''Futonorito-no-Kami''' (*太詔戸神, also called 太祝詞神* – "Great Pronouncement God") * '''Ikatsu Daijin-no-Mikoto''' (*雷大臣命, also known as Nakatomi no Ikatsu*) == '''History''' == * '''806 CE (Daidō 1st year)''': Mentioned in the '''Shinshō Kyakuchokufu-shō''' (『新抄格勅符抄』) as "Futonorito Shrine, 2 households, Tsushima Island," indicating it was granted '''two divine households''' (*shinfuko*) for its upkeep. * '''927 CE (Engi 5th year)''': Listed in the '''Engishiki Jinmyōchō''' (『延喜式神名帳』) as '''"Teita Futonorito Shrine, Myōjin Taisha"''', indicating its status as a '''Myōjin Taisha''', a high-Ranking shrine of great divine virtue. == '''Shrine Grounds''' == Within the precincts, there is a '''mound said to be the tomb of Ikatsu Daijin-no-Mikoto'''. == '''References''' == {{Reflist}} == '''Bibliography''' == - *Nagano Prefecture Place Names* (日本歴史地名大系43), Heibonsha, 2001, ISBN 4-582-49043-3 == '''Related Shrines''' == * '''Mori Shrine (Tenri City)''' – Previously known as "Futonorito Shrine." * '''Norito Shrine (Sasebo City, Nagasaki Prefecture)''' – Another shrine with a similar name. {{Commonscat|Futonorito-jinja}} {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{Shinto-stub}} * [[Nakatomi no Ikatsu]] * {{ill|Tai Norito Shrine|ja|太祝詞神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tai Norito Shrine|qq=}} in Mitsushima Town is believed to house his grave. ... 6 KB (818 words) - 23:32, 17 January 2025 }} {{translated page|ja|太祝詞神社|version=92572230|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Myōjin Taisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Tsushima]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Nagasaki Prefecture]] [[Category:Completion tier 7]] [[ja:太祝詞神社]] [[Category:Buildings in Tsushima, Nagasaki]] [[Category:History of Tsushima, Nagasaki]] {{Commons category|Futonorito-jinja}} {{wikidata link|Q11444481}} [[Category:Wikidata has short description]]

It was formerly classified as a '''Prefectural Shrine''' (*Ken-sha*) and was historically known as '''Kashi Daimyōjin''' or '''Kashi-no-Miya'''. == '''Deities Enshrined''' == The shrine venerates: * '''Futonorito-no-Kami''' (*太詔戸神, also called 太祝詞神* – "Great Pronouncement God") * '''Ikatsu Daijin-no-Mikoto''' (*雷大臣命, also known as Nakatomi no Ikatsu*) == '''History''' == * '''806 CE (Daidō 1st year)''': Mentioned in the '''Shinshō Kyakuchokufu-shō''' (『新抄格勅符抄』) as "Futonorito Shrine, 2 households, Tsushima Island," indicating it was granted '''two divine households''' (*shinfuko*) for its upkeep. * '''927 CE (Engi 5th year)''': Listed in the '''Engishiki Jinmyōchō''' (『延喜式神名帳』) as '''"Teita Futonorito Shrine, Myōjin Taisha"''', indicating its status as a '''Myōjin Taisha''', a high-Ranking shrine of great divine virtue. == '''Shrine Grounds''' == Within the precincts, there is a '''mound said to be the tomb of Ikatsu Daijin-no-Mikoto'''. == '''References''' == {{Reflist}} == '''Bibliography''' == - *Nagano Prefecture Place Names* (日本歴史地名大系43), Heibonsha, 2001, ISBN 4-582-49043-3 == '''Related Shrines''' == * '''Mori Shrine (Tenri City)''' – Previously known as "Futonorito Shrine." * '''Norito Shrine (Sasebo City, Nagasaki Prefecture)''' – Another shrine with a similar name. {{Commonscat|Futonorito-jinja}} {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{Shinto-stub}} * [[Nakatomi no Ikatsu]] * {{ill|Tai Norito Shrine|ja|太祝詞神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tai Norito Shrine|qq=}} in Mitsushima Town is believed to house his grave. ... 6 KB (818 words) - [[commons:Category:Futonorito-jinja]][[ja:太祝詞神社]] [[commons:Category:Futonorito-jinja]][[ja:太祝詞神社]] [[sn:commons:Category:Futonorito-jinja]][[ja:太祝詞神社]] {{Infobox religious building|image=File:Hutonorito jinja Haiden.JPG|prefecture={{ill|Tsushima|WD=Q991032}}|coordinates={{Coord|34.278917|N|129.239944|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}|architecture_style={{ill|nagare-zukuri|WD=Q210684}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11444481]] Wikidata name: Futonorito Shrine [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:41, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Unreferenced|date=July 20, 2018}} {{Infobox Shinto Shrine | Name = Futonorito Shrine | image = [[File:Hutonorito jinja Haiden.JPG|250px|Haiden]]
Haiden | Location = 512 Kashi, Mitsushima Town, Tsushima City, Nagasaki Prefecture, Japan | Coordinates = {{Coord|34|16|44.1|N|129|14|23.8|E|region:JP-42_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Futonorito Shrine}} | Deity = Futonorito-no-Kami (Great Pronouncement God), [[Nakatomi no Ikatsu|Ikatsu Daijin-no-Mikoto]] | Shrine Rank = Shikinaisha (Myōjin Taisha), Former Prefectural Shrine | Established = Unknown | Honden = [[Nagare-zukuri]] | Alternate Name = Kashi Daimyōjin, Kashi-no-Miya | Pilgrimage = | Annual Festival = 9th day of the 9th lunar month | Rituals = }} [[File:Futonorito jinja Gate.JPG|thumb|right|250px|Torii gate]] [[File:Futonorito jinja Honden.JPG|thumb|right|250px|Honden]] '''Futonorito Shrine''' (太祝詞神社, *Futonorito Jinja*) is a '''Shinto shrine''' located in '''Kashi, Mitsushima Town, Tsushima City, Nagasaki Prefecture'''. It was formerly classified as a '''Prefectural Shrine''' (*Ken-sha*) and was historically known as '''Kashi Daimyōjin''' or '''Kashi-no-Miya'''. == '''Deities Enshrined''' == The shrine venerates: * '''Futonorito-no-Kami''' (*太詔戸神, also called 太祝詞神* – "Great Pronouncement God") * '''Ikatsu Daijin-no-Mikoto''' (*雷大臣命, also known as Nakatomi no Ikatsu*) == '''History''' == * '''806 CE (Daidō 1st year)''': Mentioned in the '''Shinshō Kyakuchokufu-shō''' (『新抄格勅符抄』) as "Futonorito Shrine, 2 households, Tsushima Island," indicating it was granted '''two divine households''' (*shinfuko*) for its upkeep. * '''927 CE (Engi 5th year)''': Listed in the '''Engishiki Jinmyōchō''' (『延喜式神名帳』) as '''"Teita Futonorito Shrine, Myōjin Taisha"''', indicating its status as a '''Myōjin Taisha''', a high-Ranking shrine of great divine virtue. == '''Shrine Grounds''' == Within the precincts, there is a '''mound said to be the tomb of Ikatsu Daijin-no-Mikoto'''. == '''References''' == {{Reflist}} == '''Bibliography''' == - *Nagano Prefecture Place Names* (日本歴史地名大系43), Heibonsha, 2001, ISBN 4-582-49043-3 == '''Related Shrines''' == * '''Mori Shrine (Tenri City)''' – Previously known as "Futonorito Shrine." * '''Norito Shrine (Sasebo City, Nagasaki Prefecture)''' – Another shrine with a similar name. {{Commonscat|Futonorito-jinja}} {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{Shinto-stub}} * [[Nakatomi no Ikatsu]] * {{ill|Tai Norito Shrine|ja|太祝詞神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tai Norito Shrine|qq=}} in Mitsushima Town is believed to house his grave. ... 6 KB (818 words) - It was formerly classified as a '''Prefectural Shrine''' (*Ken-sha*) and was historically known as '''Kashi Daimyōjin''' or '''Kashi-no-Miya'''. == '''Deities Enshrined''' == The shrine venerates: * '''Futonorito-no-Kami''' (*太詔戸神, also called 太祝詞神* – "Great Pronouncement God") * '''Ikatsu Daijin-no-Mikoto''' (*雷大臣命, also known as Nakatomi no Ikatsu*) == '''History''' == * '''806 CE (Daidō 1st year)''': Mentioned in the '''Shinshō Kyakuchokufu-shō''' (『新抄格勅符抄』) as "Futonorito Shrine, 2 households, Tsushima Island," indicating it was granted '''two divine households''' (*shinfuko*) for its upkeep. * '''927 CE (Engi 5th year)''': Listed in the '''Engishiki Jinmyōchō''' (『延喜式神名帳』) as '''"Teita Futonorito Shrine, Myōjin Taisha"''', indicating its status as a '''Myōjin Taisha''', a high-Ranking shrine of great divine virtue. == '''Shrine Grounds''' == Within the precincts, there is a '''mound said to be the tomb of Ikatsu Daijin-no-Mikoto'''. == '''References''' == {{Reflist}} == '''Bibliography''' == - *Nagano Prefecture Place Names* (日本歴史地名大系43), Heibonsha, 2001, ISBN 4-582-49043-3 == '''Related Shrines''' == * '''Mori Shrine (Tenri City)''' – Previously known as "Futonorito Shrine." * '''Norito Shrine (Sasebo City, Nagasaki Prefecture)''' – Another shrine with a similar name. {{Commonscat|Futonorito-jinja}} {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{Shinto-stub}} * [[Nakatomi no Ikatsu]] * {{ill|Tai Norito Shrine|ja|太祝詞神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tai Norito Shrine|qq=}} in Mitsushima Town is believed to house his grave. ... 6 KB (818 words) - [[co:commons:Category:Futonorito-jinja]][[ja:太祝詞神社]] [[co:commons:Category:Futonorito-jinja]][[ja:太祝詞神社]] [[Category:sn_crud|TESTcommons:Category:Futonorito-jinja]][[ja:太祝詞神社]] {{Infobox religious building|image=File:Hutonorito jinja Haiden.JPG|prefecture={{ill|Tsushima|WD=Q991032|1=Tsushima, Nagasaki}}|coordinates={{Coord|34.278917|N|129.239944|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}|architecture_style={{ill|nagare-zukuri|WD=Q210684|1=Nagare-zukuri}}}} {{Unreferenced|date=July 20, 2018}} {{Infobox Shinto Shrine | Name = Futonorito Shrine | image = [[File:Hutonorito jinja Haiden.JPG|250px|Haiden]]
Haiden | Location = 512 Kashi, Mitsushima Town, Tsushima City, Nagasaki Prefecture, Japan | Coordinates = {{Coord|34|16|44.1|N|129|14|23.8|E|region:JP-42_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Futonorito Shrine}} | Deity = Futonorito-no-Kami (Great Pronouncement God), [[Nakatomi no Ikatsu|Ikatsu Daijin-no-Mikoto]] | Shrine Rank = Shikinaisha (Myōjin Taisha), Former Prefectural Shrine | Established = Unknown | Honden = [[Nagare-zukuri]] | Alternate Name = Kashi Daimyōjin, Kashi-no-Miya | Pilgrimage = | Annual Festival = 9th day of the 9th lunar month | Rituals = }} [[File:Futonorito jinja Gate.JPG|thumb|right|250px|Torii gate]] [[File:Futonorito jinja Honden.JPG|thumb|right|250px|Honden]] '''Futonorito Shrine''' (太祝詞神社, *Futonorito Jinja*) is a '''Shinto shrine''' located in '''Kashi, Mitsushima Town, Tsushima City, Nagasaki Prefecture'''. It was formerly classified as a '''Prefectural Shrine''' (*Ken-sha*) and was historically known as '''Kashi Daimyōjin''' or '''Kashi-no-Miya'''. == '''Deities Enshrined''' == The shrine venerates: * '''Futonorito-no-Kami''' (*太詔戸神, also called 太祝詞神* – "Great Pronouncement God") * '''Ikatsu Daijin-no-Mikoto''' (*雷大臣命, also known as Nakatomi no Ikatsu*) == '''History''' == * '''806 CE (Daidō 1st year)''': Mentioned in the '''Shinshō Kyakuchokufu-shō''' (『新抄格勅符抄』) as "Futonorito Shrine, 2 households, Tsushima Island," indicating it was granted '''two divine households''' (*shinfuko*) for its upkeep. * '''927 CE (Engi 5th year)''': Listed in the '''Engishiki Jinmyōchō''' (『延喜式神名帳』) as '''"Teita Futonorito Shrine, Myōjin Taisha"''', indicating its status as a '''Myōjin Taisha''', a high-Ranking shrine of great divine virtue. == '''Shrine Grounds''' == Within the precincts, there is a '''mound said to be the tomb of Ikatsu Daijin-no-Mikoto'''. == '''References''' == {{Reflist}} == '''Bibliography''' == - *Nagano Prefecture Place Names* (日本歴史地名大系43), Heibonsha, 2001, ISBN 4-582-49043-3 == '''Related Shrines''' == * '''Mori Shrine (Tenri City)''' – Previously known as "Futonorito Shrine." * '''Norito Shrine (Sasebo City, Nagasaki Prefecture)''' – Another shrine with a similar name. {{Commonscat|Futonorito-jinja}} {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{Shinto-stub}} * [[Nakatomi no Ikatsu]] * {{ill|Tai Norito Shrine|ja|太祝詞神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tai Norito Shrine|qq=}} in Mitsushima Town is believed to house his grave. ... 6 KB (818 words) - 23:32, 17 January 2025 }} {{translated page|ja|太祝詞神社|version=92572230|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[ja:太祝詞神社]] {{Commons category|Futonorito-jinja}} {{wikidata link|Q11444481}} [[Category:Buildings and structures in Tsushima, Nagasaki]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Nagasaki Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Tsushima, Nagasaki]] [[Category:Myōjin Taisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Tsushima]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Myōjin Taisha]]
